english_meaning: definite article
definition_simple: Used before a noun to refer to a specific person or thing.
definition_detailed: The definite article 'the' is the most common word in English. It precedes nouns to indicate that the noun refers to something specific, unique, or previously mentioned. It works with singular, plural, and uncountable nouns.
example_sentence: The sun rises in the east every morning.
example_sentence_hindi: सूरज हर सुबह पूरब में उगता है।

english_meaning: to exist or have a state
definition_simple: To exist, live, or occupy a position; also used as a linking or auxiliary verb.
definition_detailed: 'Be' is the most versatile verb in English. It functions as a main verb meaning to exist, as a linking verb connecting subject to complement, and as an auxiliary verb forming tenses and passives. Its forms include am, is, are, was, were, been, and being.
example_sentence: She will be at the office tomorrow.
example_sentence_hindi: वह कल ऑफिस में होगी।

english_meaning: indicating direction, purpose, or infinitive
definition_simple: A preposition showing direction, relationship, or used before an infinitive verb.
definition_detailed: 'To' is a multi-purpose word in English. As a preposition it indicates direction, destination, or a relationship. As an infinitive marker it precedes base-form verbs. It is the third most common word in English and appears in countless constructions.
example_sentence: I want to learn English well.
example_sentence_hindi: मैं अच्छी तरह अंग्रेजी सीखना चाहता हूं।

english_meaning: expressing relationship or belonging
definition_simple: Indicates relationship, belonging, or that something is part of something else.
definition_detailed: 'Of' is the most common preposition in English, expressing a wide range of relationships including possession, origin, material composition, and membership. It connects nouns to other nouns and is essential in forming noun phrases.
example_sentence: The color of the sky is blue.
example_sentence_hindi: आकाश का रंग नीला है।

english_meaning: used to connect words, phrases, or clauses
definition_simple: A conjunction that joins words, phrases, or clauses together.
definition_detailed: 'And' is a coordinating conjunction that joins two or more elements of equal grammatical status. It is used to add information, combine ideas, or indicate that two things occur together. It is the fifth most common word in English.
example_sentence: She bought apples and oranges from the market.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने बाज़ार से सेब और संतरे खरीदे।

english_meaning: indefinite article used before consonant sounds
definition_simple: Used before a singular noun to refer to any one of that thing.
definition_detailed: 'A' is the indefinite article used before singular nouns that begin with a consonant sound. It indicates that the noun is non-specific or is being mentioned for the first time. It is used to classify or introduce new information.
example_sentence: She is reading a book about history.
example_sentence_hindi: वह इतिहास के बारे में एक किताब पढ़ रही है।

english_meaning: within a place, time, or state
definition_simple: Indicates location inside something, or inclusion in a time or group.
definition_detailed: 'In' is a preposition indicating containment, location within boundaries, inclusion in a group or period, or a state. It is one of the most common prepositions in English and is used in a vast number of expressions and phrasal verbs.
example_sentence: She lives in a small town near the river.
example_sentence_hindi: वह नदी के पास एक छोटे शहर में रहती है।

english_meaning: used as conjunction, pronoun, or demonstrative determiner
definition_simple: Used to introduce a clause, refer to a specific thing, or point to something.
definition_detailed: 'That' serves multiple grammatical roles. As a conjunction it introduces subordinate clauses. As a demonstrative pronoun or determiner it points to something specific, usually farther away. As a relative pronoun it introduces relative clauses identifying which person or thing is meant.
example_sentence: She said that she would come tomorrow.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने कहा कि वह कल आएगी।

english_meaning: to possess or experience; auxiliary for perfect tenses
definition_simple: To own or possess something; also used as an auxiliary verb to form perfect tenses.
definition_detailed: 'Have' functions both as a main verb expressing possession, experience, or obligation, and as an auxiliary verb forming perfect tenses (have done, had done). It is one of the most frequent verbs in English and appears in dozens of fixed expressions and phrasal verbs.
example_sentence: I have lived here for ten years.
example_sentence_hindi: मैं यहाँ दस साल से रह रहा हूं।

english_meaning: third-person singular neuter pronoun
definition_simple: Refers to a thing, animal, or situation previously mentioned or about to be mentioned.
definition_detailed: 'It' is the third-person singular neuter pronoun, used to refer to things, animals (when gender is unknown), ideas, situations, or as a dummy subject in impersonal constructions (e.g., 'It is raining'). It is the tenth most common word in English.
example_sentence: It is raining heavily outside today.
example_sentence_hindi: आज बाहर बहुत तेज बारिश हो रही है।

english_meaning: indicating purpose, duration, or beneficiary
definition_simple: Used to show purpose, intended recipient, or a length of time.
definition_detailed: 'For' is a versatile preposition expressing purpose ('I did it for you'), duration ('for three hours'), destination ('leave for Paris'), exchange ('traded for cash'), and reason. It also functions as a conjunction meaning 'because' in formal writing.
example_sentence: She studied hard for her final exams.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने अपनी अंतिम परीक्षाओं के लिए कड़ी मेहनत की।

english_meaning: used to negate a verb or adjective
definition_simple: Used to make a verb, adjective, or clause negative.
definition_detailed: 'Not' is the primary negation adverb in English. It is placed after auxiliary verbs or the verb 'be' to negate the sentence. It can also negate adjectives, nouns, and phrases. In informal speech it contracts with auxiliaries: isn't, can't, won't, don't, etc.
example_sentence: She did not want to miss the meeting.
example_sentence_hindi: वह बैठक छोड़ना नहीं चाहती थी।

english_meaning: indicating surface contact, topics, or time
definition_simple: Indicates that something is on a surface, or refers to a topic, day, or state.
definition_detailed: 'On' is a common preposition with many uses: position on a surface ('on the table'), time references ('on Monday'), topics ('on the subject'), and media ('on TV'). It also forms many phrasal verbs and fixed expressions.
example_sentence: The book is on the table near the window.
example_sentence_hindi: किताब खिड़की के पास मेज पर है।

english_meaning: accompanied by or using
definition_simple: Indicates accompaniment, manner, or the use of something.
definition_detailed: 'With' is a preposition that expresses accompaniment ('come with me'), means or instrument ('cut with a knife'), manner ('with care'), and association. It forms many phrasal verbs and fixed expressions. Its antonym 'without' means lacking accompaniment.
example_sentence: She went to school with her younger brother.
example_sentence_hindi: वह अपने छोटे भाई के साथ स्कूल गई।

english_meaning: third-person singular masculine pronoun
definition_simple: Refers to a male person or animal previously mentioned.
definition_detailed: 'He' is the third-person singular masculine subject pronoun in English. It is used to refer to males (and sometimes animals when gender is known). Its related forms are 'him' (object), 'his' (possessive), and 'himself' (reflexive).
example_sentence: He is a very talented musician and composer.
example_sentence_hindi: वह एक बहुत प्रतिभाशाली संगीतकार और गायक है।

english_meaning: in the same way, manner, or degree; while; because
definition_simple: Used to express comparison, simultaneous actions, or reason.
definition_detailed: 'As' is a highly flexible word in English. It functions as a conjunction of comparison ('as fast as'), time ('as I was leaving'), reason ('as you know'), and manner ('do as I say'). It also works as a preposition in roles ('served as captain') and as an adverb in comparisons.
example_sentence: She spoke as clearly as she possibly could.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने जितना हो सके उतना स्पष्ट रूप से बोला।

english_meaning: second-person pronoun
definition_simple: Refers to the person or people being spoken to.
definition_detailed: 'You' is the second-person pronoun in English, used for both singular and plural, and for formal and informal registers. Unlike many languages, English uses a single form 'you' for all cases. Its related forms include 'your' (possessive), 'yours', and 'yourself/yourselves' (reflexive).
example_sentence: You should always believe in yourself.
example_sentence_hindi: तुम्हें हमेशा खुद पर विश्वास रखना चाहिए।

english_meaning: to perform an action; auxiliary for questions and negation
definition_simple: To perform or carry out an action; also used as auxiliary for questions.
definition_detailed: 'Do' is both a main verb meaning to perform or carry out an action, and an auxiliary verb used to form questions, negatives, and emphatic statements. As a main verb it is highly versatile. As an auxiliary it appears in constructions like 'Do you know?', 'She doesn't like', and 'I do agree'.
example_sentence: What do you do for work every day?
example_sentence_hindi: तुम हर दिन काम के लिए क्या करते हो?

english_meaning: indicating a specific location or time
definition_simple: Used to indicate a precise location, time, or direction toward something.
definition_detailed: 'At' is a preposition primarily used for precise location ('at the corner'), specific times ('at 5 o'clock'), and direction ('look at me'). It appears in many fixed expressions and phrasal verbs. It contrasts with 'in' (enclosed spaces) and 'on' (surfaces).
example_sentence: We will meet at the library at three o'clock.
example_sentence_hindi: हम तीन बजे पुस्तकालय में मिलेंगे।

english_meaning: used to refer to something nearby or just mentioned
definition_simple: Points to a specific nearby person, thing, or idea just mentioned.
definition_detailed: 'This' is a demonstrative determiner and pronoun used to indicate something nearby in space or time, or something just mentioned. It contrasts with 'that', which refers to something farther away. In discourse it introduces new information or points back to what was just said.
example_sentence: This new technology will change the world.
example_sentence_hindi: यह नई तकनीक दुनिया बदल देगी।

english_meaning: introducing contrast or exception
definition_simple: Used to introduce a contrast, exception, or unexpected information.
definition_detailed: 'But' is a coordinating conjunction that introduces a contrasting idea, an exception, or something unexpected. It signals that what follows differs from or qualifies what came before. It can also mean 'except' ('everyone but me') or 'only' in formal contexts.
example_sentence: She tried hard, but she could not finish the test.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने बहुत कोशिश की, लेकिन वह परीक्षा खत्म नहीं कर सकी।

english_meaning: belonging to a male person
definition_simple: Possessive determiner or pronoun referring to a male.
definition_detailed: 'His' serves as both a possessive determiner ('his book') and a possessive pronoun ('the book is his'). It is the masculine third-person singular possessive, related to 'he' and 'him'. It can refer to males or to animals of known male gender.
example_sentence: His performance in the exam was outstanding.
example_sentence_hindi: परीक्षा में उसका प्रदर्शन उत्कृष्ट था।

english_meaning: indicating agent, means, proximity, or deadline
definition_simple: Used to show who did something, how it was done, or nearness to a place or time.
definition_detailed: 'By' is a preposition with several uses: it indicates the agent in passive constructions ('written by her'), means or method ('travel by train'), proximity ('sit by the fire'), and deadline ('finish by Friday'). It also appears in many idiomatic expressions.
example_sentence: The novel was written by a famous author.
example_sentence_hindi: यह उपन्यास एक प्रसिद्ध लेखक द्वारा लिखा गया था।

english_meaning: indicating origin, starting point, or source
definition_simple: Shows where something or someone originated or started.
definition_detailed: 'From' is a preposition indicating origin ('from India'), starting point in time or space ('from morning to night'), source or cause ('learn from experience'), and separation ('take from the shelf'). It expresses the beginning of a movement or range.
example_sentence: She traveled all the way from Paris to London.
example_sentence_hindi: वह पेरिस से लंदन तक की यात्रा की।

english_meaning: third-person plural pronoun or singular gender-neutral pronoun
definition_simple: Refers to multiple people or things previously mentioned; also used as a singular gender-neutral pronoun.
definition_detailed: 'They' is the third-person plural subject pronoun, referring to multiple people or things. In modern usage it is also increasingly accepted as a singular pronoun for non-binary individuals or when gender is unknown or irrelevant ('someone called — they left a message').
example_sentence: They decided to start the meeting early.
example_sentence_hindi: उन्होंने बैठक जल्दी शुरू करने का फैसला किया।

english_meaning: first-person plural pronoun
definition_simple: Refers to the speaker and one or more other people.
definition_detailed: 'We' is the first-person plural subject pronoun, referring to the speaker and one or more other people. Its object form is 'us', its possessive is 'our/ours', and its reflexive is 'ourselves'. It expresses shared identity, community, or collective action.
example_sentence: We are planning a trip to the mountains.
example_sentence_hindi: हम पहाड़ों की यात्रा की योजना बना रहे हैं।

english_meaning: to speak or express in words
definition_simple: To speak words or express something verbally.
definition_detailed: 'Say' is a common verb of speech meaning to utter words, express a thought, or state something. It is used in direct speech ('She said hello'), indirect speech ('He said that he was tired'), and many idiomatic expressions ('say the word', 'I say'). It differs from 'tell', which requires an object.
example_sentence: She always says what she truly thinks.
example_sentence_hindi: वह हमेशा वही कहती है जो वह सच में सोचती है।

english_meaning: object form or possessive of she
definition_simple: Used as the object of a verb or as a possessive referring to a female.
definition_detailed: 'Her' serves as the object pronoun for 'she' ('I saw her') and as the possessive determiner ('her book'). It is the feminine third-person singular and is one of the most common pronouns in English. The possessive pronoun form is 'hers'.
example_sentence: I gave her the book she wanted to read.
example_sentence_hindi: मैंने उसे वह किताब दी जो वह पढ़ना चाहती थी।

english_meaning: third-person singular feminine pronoun
definition_simple: Refers to a female person or animal previously mentioned.
definition_detailed: 'She' is the third-person singular feminine subject pronoun. It refers to female persons, animals of known female gender, and in poetic or traditional usage to ships, countries, and nature. Its related forms are 'her' (object/possessive), 'hers' (possessive pronoun), and 'herself' (reflexive).
example_sentence: She passed all her exams with excellent grades.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने सभी परीक्षाएं बहुत अच्छे अंकों से पास कीं।

english_meaning: presenting an alternative or choice
definition_simple: Used to connect alternatives or indicate a choice between things.
definition_detailed: 'Or' is a coordinating conjunction that presents alternatives ('tea or coffee'), expresses uncertainty ('is it Monday or Tuesday?'), and introduces a consequence ('hurry or you'll be late'). In logic it functions as the inclusive disjunction operator.
example_sentence: Would you like tea or coffee this morning?
example_sentence_hindi: क्या आप इस सुबह चाय या कॉफी लेना चाहेंगे?

english_meaning: indefinite article used before vowel sounds
definition_simple: Used before singular nouns that begin with a vowel sound.
definition_detailed: 'An' is the form of the indefinite article 'a' used before words that begin with a vowel sound. The choice between 'a' and 'an' depends on pronunciation, not spelling: 'an hour' (silent h), 'a university' (consonant sound). It indicates a non-specific single item.
example_sentence: She is an excellent teacher with years of experience.
example_sentence_hindi: वह एक उत्कृष्ट शिक्षिका है जिसके पास वर्षों का अनुभव है।

english_meaning: modal auxiliary expressing future or willingness
definition_simple: Used to express future actions, predictions, promises, or willingness.
definition_detailed: 'Will' is a modal auxiliary verb with several core uses: expressing future time ('I will go'), making predictions ('It will rain'), expressing willingness or determination ('I will do it'), and making promises or offers. Its past/conditional form is 'would'.
example_sentence: She will finish the project before the deadline.
example_sentence_hindi: वह समय सीमा से पहले प्रोजेक्ट खत्म कर लेगी।

english_meaning: belonging to the speaker
definition_simple: Possessive determiner indicating something belongs to the speaker.
definition_detailed: 'My' is the first-person singular possessive determiner. It indicates that the following noun belongs to or is associated with the speaker. Its related forms are 'mine' (possessive pronoun) and 'myself' (reflexive). It is one of the most frequent possessive words in English.
example_sentence: My family always gathers for dinner on Sundays.
example_sentence_hindi: मेरा परिवार रविवार को हमेशा रात के खाने के लिए इकट्ठा होता है।

english_meaning: the number 1; a single person or thing
definition_simple: The number 1; also used as a pronoun meaning a person in general.
definition_detailed: 'One' is the cardinal number 1. It is used to count single items, refer to a particular person or thing ('one of them'), and as a generic pronoun in formal or impersonal statements ('one should be careful'). It is also used idiomatically in many expressions.
example_sentence: One good idea can change everything completely.
example_sentence_hindi: एक अच्छा विचार सब कुछ पूरी तरह बदल सकता है।

english_meaning: the whole amount, quantity, or extent
definition_simple: Refers to the whole of a group, quantity, or extent; every member included.
definition_detailed: 'All' is a predeterminer, determiner, and pronoun meaning the complete or whole amount of something. It can precede the, my, their etc. ('all the students'), function as a standalone pronoun ('all is well'), or precede adjectives as an intensifier ('all alone'). It emphasizes totality.
example_sentence: All students must attend the final examination.
example_sentence_hindi: सभी छात्रों को अंतिम परीक्षा में उपस्थित होना होगा।

english_meaning: past tense of will; used for conditions and polite requests
definition_simple: Used for polite requests, hypothetical situations, or past habits.
definition_detailed: 'Would' is the past tense of 'will' and serves several functions: expressing conditional statements ('I would help if I could'), making polite requests ('Would you pass the salt?'), describing past habits ('We would visit every summer'), and expressing preference ('I would rather stay').
example_sentence: Would you like to join us for dinner tonight?
example_sentence_hindi: क्या आप आज रात हमारे साथ रात के खाने पर आना चाहेंगे?

english_meaning: in, at, or to that place; also existential marker
definition_simple: Refers to a place or location; also used to introduce a sentence.
definition_detailed: 'There' functions as an adverb of place ('put it there'), a demonstrative pointing to something ('there it is'), and as the dummy subject in existential sentences ('There is a problem'). Its existential use is extremely common in English to introduce new information.
example_sentence: There is a beautiful park near our house.
example_sentence_hindi: हमारे घर के पास एक सुंदर पार्क है।

english_meaning: possessive form of they
definition_simple: Belonging to or associated with the people or things mentioned.
definition_detailed: 'Their' is the third-person plural possessive determiner, indicating that something belongs to a group. In modern usage it is also used as a singular gender-neutral possessive ('everyone should do their best'). Its related pronoun form is 'theirs'.
example_sentence: Their team won the national championship last year.
example_sentence_hindi: उनकी टीम ने पिछले साल राष्ट्रीय चैम्पियनशिप जीती।

english_meaning: asking about or referring to something unknown
definition_simple: Used to ask questions about things or introduce indirect questions.
definition_detailed: 'What' is an interrogative pronoun and determiner used to ask about the identity or nature of things ('What is your name?'), to introduce indirect questions ('I don't know what happened'), and in exclamations ('What a beautiful day!'). It is one of the core question words in English.
example_sentence: What are you planning to do this weekend?
example_sentence_hindi: इस सप्ताहांत आप क्या करने की योजना बना रहे हैं?

english_meaning: to a great degree; therefore; introducing result
definition_simple: Used to show degree, result, or to continue a conversation.
definition_detailed: 'So' has multiple functions: as an adverb of degree ('so beautiful'), as a conjunction of result ('she worked hard, so she passed'), as an adverb meaning 'also' ('I do so'), and as an informal discourse marker ('So, what happened?'). It is extremely versatile in spoken English.
example_sentence: The weather was so cold that we stayed inside.
example_sentence_hindi: मौसम इतना ठंडा था कि हम घर के अंदर रहे।

english_meaning: toward a higher position or level
definition_simple: Moving or directed toward a higher position; increasing or rising.
definition_detailed: 'Up' primarily indicates movement to a higher position or level. As an adverb it modifies verbs ('stand up', 'look up'). As a preposition it indicates movement along a surface upward ('up the stairs'). It is also used in phrasal verbs with a wide range of meanings ('give up', 'make up', 'pick up').
example_sentence: She looked up at the stars in the clear sky.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने साफ आसमान में तारों की ओर ऊपर देखा।

english_meaning: away from the inside or center
definition_simple: Away from a place, position, or state; away from the interior.
definition_detailed: 'Out' indicates movement away from inside to outside, visibility ('find out', 'come out'), removal ('take out'), and completion ('run out', 'work out'). It forms many phrasal verbs with very different meanings. As an adjective it means 'not in' or 'not available'.
example_sentence: She walked out of the office without saying a word.
example_sentence_hindi: वह बिना कुछ कहे कार्यालय से बाहर चली गई।

english_meaning: introducing a condition or hypothesis
definition_simple: Used to introduce a conditional clause or a hypothetical situation.
definition_detailed: 'If' is a subordinating conjunction that introduces conditional clauses. It is used in real conditions ('If it rains, take an umbrella'), hypothetical conditions ('If I were rich, I would travel'), indirect questions ('Ask if she is coming'), and exclamations ('If only I had known!'). It is fundamental to conditional logic in English.
example_sentence: If you study hard, you will pass the exam.
example_sentence_hindi: अगर आप कड़ी मेहनत करते हैं, तो आप परीक्षा पास करेंगे।

english_meaning: on the subject of; approximately
definition_simple: Relating to a topic or roughly at a quantity.
definition_detailed: 'About' functions as a preposition ('talk about the problem'), an adverb meaning approximately ('about ten people'), and in expressions of concern ('what about me?'). It is commonly used to indicate the subject matter of a conversation, book, or thought.
example_sentence: The documentary is about climate change and its effects.
example_sentence_hindi: यह वृत्तचित्र जलवायु परिवर्तन और उसके प्रभावों के बारे में है।

english_meaning: interrogative or relative pronoun referring to a person
definition_simple: Used to ask which person or to introduce a clause about a person.
definition_detailed: 'Who' is an interrogative pronoun used in questions about the identity of a person ('Who is calling?'), and a relative pronoun introducing relative clauses ('the person who helped me'). Its object form is 'whom' (formal), its possessive is 'whose', and its inclusive form is 'whoever'.
example_sentence: Who is the person responsible for this project?
example_sentence_hindi: इस परियोजना के लिए जिम्मेदार व्यक्ति कौन है?

english_meaning: to receive, obtain, or become
definition_simple: To receive, obtain, or become something; highly versatile verb.
definition_detailed: 'Get' is one of the most versatile verbs in English. It expresses receiving ('get a gift'), obtaining ('get help'), movement ('get home'), becoming ('get angry'), causation ('get someone to do something'), and is used in over 100 phrasal verbs. It is especially common in informal English.
example_sentence: She managed to get tickets for the concert.
example_sentence_hindi: वह संगीत समारोह के लिए टिकट प्राप्त करने में सफल रही।

english_meaning: asking which one or introducing a relative clause
definition_simple: Used to identify a specific item from a group or introduce a relative clause.
definition_detailed: 'Which' is both an interrogative pronoun/determiner ('Which road leads to the city?') and a relative pronoun ('the car which I bought'). As an interrogative it implies choosing between known options, unlike 'what' which is more open-ended. As a relative pronoun it typically refers to things rather than people.
example_sentence: Which of these books would you recommend reading first?
example_sentence_hindi: इनमें से कौन सी किताब आप पहले पढ़ने की सिफारिश करेंगे?

english_meaning: to move from one place to another; to proceed
definition_simple: To move, travel, or proceed from one place to another.
definition_detailed: 'Go' is a highly common verb expressing movement away from a point, travel to a destination, and in many phrasal verbs and expressions. It also functions as a linking verb ('things go wrong') and as a future construction ('going to'). Its irregular past tense 'went' is noteworthy.
example_sentence: Let's go to the beach this Saturday afternoon.
example_sentence_hindi: इस शनिवार दोपहर समुद्र तट पर चलते हैं।

english_meaning: object form of I; referring to the speaker
definition_simple: Used as the object of a verb or preposition to refer to the speaker.
definition_detailed: 'Me' is the first-person singular object pronoun. It is used as the direct object ('She helped me'), indirect object ('Give me a chance'), object of a preposition ('for me'), and in informal constructions as a subject equivalent ('It's me'). As a reflexive, 'myself' is used.
example_sentence: Please give me a moment to think carefully.
example_sentence_hindi: कृपया मुझे सोचने के लिए एक पल दें।

english_meaning: at what time; at the time that
definition_simple: Used to ask about or state the time something happens.
definition_detailed: 'When' is an interrogative adverb ('When did you arrive?'), a relative adverb in relative clauses ('the day when we met'), and a subordinating conjunction in time clauses ('When she arrived, we started'). It is one of the core question words in English.
example_sentence: When will you be able to come to the party?
example_sentence_hindi: आप पार्टी में कब आ पाएंगे?

english_meaning: to create, produce, or cause something
definition_simple: To create, produce, or cause something to happen.
definition_detailed: 'Make' is one of the most common verbs in English with many meanings: to create ('make a cake'), to cause ('make someone happy'), to earn ('make money'), and to perform ('make a decision'). It contrasts with 'do' — generally 'make' is used for creating or producing.
example_sentence: She makes delicious food for her family every day.
example_sentence_hindi: वह हर रोज़ अपने परिवार के लिए स्वादिष्ट खाना बनाती है।

english_meaning: expressing ability or possibility
definition_simple: Used to express ability, permission, or possibility.
definition_detailed: 'Can' is a modal auxiliary verb expressing ability ('I can swim'), possibility ('That can happen'), permission in informal contexts ('Can I go?'), and general capacity. Its past tense is 'could'. It contrasts with 'may' which is more formal for permission.
example_sentence: She can speak three languages very fluently.
example_sentence_hindi: वह बहुत धाराप्रवाह तीन भाषाएं बोल सकती है।

english_meaning: to find something pleasant; similar to something
definition_simple: To find something pleasant or enjoyable; also used for comparison.
definition_detailed: 'Like' as a verb expresses preference or enjoyment ('I like music'). As a preposition or conjunction it means 'similar to' ('runs like the wind'). In informal speech it serves as a discourse marker and quotative ('She was like, 'Wow''). It is extremely common in everyday English.
example_sentence: She likes reading novels and listening to classical music.
example_sentence_hindi: वह उपन्यास पढ़ना और शास्त्रीय संगीत सुनना पसंद करती है।

english_meaning: the indefinite continued progress of events; a moment or period
definition_simple: The progress of events from past to future; a specific point or period.
definition_detailed: 'Time' refers to the continuous passing of events from the past through the present to the future. As a countable noun it refers to specific occasions ('many times'). It is one of the most conceptually important words in human language and appears in hundreds of expressions and compound words.
example_sentence: Time is the most valuable resource we have.
example_sentence_hindi: समय हमारे पास सबसे मूल्यवान संसाधन है।

english_meaning: used to deny, refuse, or express the negative
definition_simple: Used to express denial, disagreement, or the absence of something.
definition_detailed: 'No' functions as an adverb of negation ('No, I don't agree'), a determiner meaning 'not any' ('No time left'), and a noun in some constructions ('Give me a yes or a no'). It is one of the most fundamental response words in English.
example_sentence: No, I do not think that is the right answer.
example_sentence_hindi: नहीं, मुझे नहीं लगता कि यह सही उत्तर है।

english_meaning: exactly; only; very recently
definition_simple: Used to mean exactly, only, or that something happened very recently.
definition_detailed: 'Just' has several common uses: meaning 'exactly' ('just right'), 'only' ('just a moment'), 'very recently' ('I just arrived'), and as an intensifier in informal speech ('I just love it'). It can also express fairness as an adjective ('a just decision') from the same Latin root as 'justice'.
example_sentence: She just finished writing the report a moment ago.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने बस अभी कुछ देर पहले रिपोर्ट लिखना खत्म किया।

english_meaning: object form of he
definition_simple: Used as the object of a verb or preposition referring to a male.
definition_detailed: 'Him' is the third-person singular masculine object pronoun. It is used as the direct object ('I saw him'), indirect object ('I gave him the book'), and object of a preposition ('for him'). Its subject form is 'he' and its possessive is 'his'.
example_sentence: She told him the truth about what had happened.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने उसे बताया कि क्या हुआ था।

english_meaning: to have information or awareness about something
definition_simple: To have knowledge, awareness, or understanding of something.
definition_detailed: 'Know' means to have information ('I know the answer'), be acquainted with ('I know her'), recognize ('I know that smell'), and understand ('I know what you mean'). It is a stative verb, so it is not normally used in progressive tenses. Its irregular past tense is 'knew'.
example_sentence: She knows exactly how to solve this problem.
example_sentence_hindi: वह जानती है कि इस समस्या को कैसे हल करना है।

english_meaning: to hold, carry, or move something; to require
definition_simple: To grasp and carry something away; to require time or effort.
definition_detailed: 'Take' is a highly versatile verb meaning to grasp ('take a pen'), carry away ('take it home'), require ('it takes time'), accept ('take advice'), travel by ('take the bus'), and perform ('take a photo'). It forms over 100 phrasal verbs and idioms in English.
example_sentence: It takes a lot of courage to admit your mistakes.
example_sentence_hindi: अपनी गलतियाँ स्वीकार करने में बहुत साहस चाहिए।

english_meaning: human beings in general; a group of persons
definition_simple: Human beings collectively; a group of men, women, and children.
definition_detailed: 'People' is the plural of 'person' in most contexts and refers to human beings in general ('people are kind') or a specific group ('the people of France'). As a noun it can be preceded by numbers ('five people') and quantifiers. The form 'peoples' refers to distinct ethnic groups.
example_sentence: People from all over the world attended the conference.
example_sentence_hindi: दुनिया भर के लोगों ने सम्मेलन में भाग लिया।

english_meaning: to the inside of; in the direction of
definition_simple: Indicates movement to the interior of something or entry into a state.
definition_detailed: 'Into' indicates movement from outside to inside ('walked into the room'), change of state ('turned into a butterfly'), contact ('bumped into him'), or a point of entry. It always implies motion or change, unlike 'in' which indicates static position.
example_sentence: She walked into the room and turned on the light.
example_sentence_hindi: वह कमरे में आई और लाइट जलाई।

english_meaning: a period of 12 months
definition_simple: A period of 365 or 366 days; twelve calendar months.
definition_detailed: 'Year' refers to the period of approximately 365 days it takes Earth to orbit the Sun, or any 12-month period. It is used to measure duration ('for five years'), denote periods in time ('the year 2000'), and in many temporal expressions. The plural 'years' indicates extended duration.
example_sentence: She has been working at the same company for ten years.
example_sentence_hindi: वह दस सालों से एक ही कंपनी में काम कर रही है।

english_meaning: belonging to or associated with the person being spoken to
definition_simple: Possessive determiner referring to what belongs to the person being addressed.
definition_detailed: 'Your' is the second-person possessive determiner, indicating that something belongs to or is associated with the person being spoken to. It works for both singular and plural 'you'. The corresponding possessive pronoun is 'yours' and reflexive is 'yourself/yourselves'.
example_sentence: Your dedication and hard work will bring great results.
example_sentence_hindi: आपकी लगन और कड़ी मेहनत अच्छे परिणाम लाएगी।

english_meaning: of a favorable quality or character
definition_simple: Of a positive quality; right, suitable, or beneficial.
definition_detailed: 'Good' is one of the most common adjectives in English, expressing positive quality ('good food'), moral virtue ('a good person'), skill ('good at math'), benefit ('good for you'), and used as an exclamation ('Good!'). Its comparative is 'better' and superlative is 'best', both irregular.
example_sentence: She gave a very good performance in the school play.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने स्कूल के नाटक में बहुत अच्छा प्रदर्शन किया।

english_meaning: an unspecified amount or number
definition_simple: An unspecified quantity; a part of a whole.
definition_detailed: 'Some' indicates an unspecified but positive quantity ('some water', 'some people'). As a determiner it precedes uncountable or plural nouns. As a pronoun it stands alone ('Can I have some?'). It contrasts with 'any', which is used in questions and negatives.
example_sentence: She brought some flowers from the garden for the table.
example_sentence_hindi: वह बगीचे से मेज के लिए कुछ फूल लाई।

english_meaning: past tense of can; used for possibility or polite request
definition_simple: Past tense of can; expresses past ability, polite requests, or possibility.
definition_detailed: 'Could' is the past tense of 'can' and serves several functions: expressing past ability ('I could run fast as a child'), possibility ('It could rain'), polite requests ('Could you help me?'), conditional ('If I could, I would'), and suggestions ('You could try again').
example_sentence: She could speak French when she was in Paris.
example_sentence_hindi: जब वह पेरिस में थी तो वह फ्रेंच बोल सकती थी।

english_meaning: object form of they
definition_simple: Object pronoun for they; referring to multiple people or things.
definition_detailed: 'Them' is the third-person plural object pronoun, used as the direct object ('I saw them'), indirect object ('I gave them the book'), and object of a preposition ('for them'). In modern usage it also serves as a singular gender-neutral object pronoun.
example_sentence: She introduced them to her parents last evening.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने कल शाम उन्हें अपने माता-पिता से मिलवाया।

english_meaning: to perceive with the eyes; to understand
definition_simple: To perceive visually; also to understand or encounter.
definition_detailed: 'See' primarily means to perceive with the eyes ('I can see the mountains'), and also means to understand ('I see what you mean'), to meet ('I'll see you tomorrow'), to find out ('Let's see what happens'), and to escort ('I'll see you to the door'). Its irregular past tense is 'saw'.
example_sentence: Can you see the mountains from your window today?
example_sentence_hindi: क्या आप आज अपनी खिड़की से पहाड़ देख सकते हैं?

english_meaning: different from the one already mentioned; additional
definition_simple: Not the same as the one mentioned; additional or alternative.
definition_detailed: 'Other' means different from or additional to what has been mentioned. As a determiner ('the other day'), pronoun ('the others'), and adjective ('other options'), it indicates something distinct from what is already specified. 'Another' is the singular form and 'others' the plural pronoun.
example_sentence: She found a completely different solution from the other team.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने दूसरी टीम से बिल्कुल अलग समाधान खोजा।

english_meaning: used in comparisons to introduce the second element
definition_simple: Used after comparative adjectives or adverbs to introduce what is being compared.
definition_detailed: 'Than' is a conjunction or preposition used in comparisons after comparative adjectives and adverbs ('faster than', 'more beautiful than'). It signals the second element of a comparison. It should not be confused with 'then' which relates to time.
example_sentence: She runs much faster than anyone else in the class.
example_sentence_hindi: वह कक्षा में किसी से भी बहुत तेज दौड़ती है।

english_meaning: at that time; next in sequence
definition_simple: At that time; after that; as a consequence.
definition_detailed: 'Then' functions as a temporal adverb ('I was young then'), a sequential connector ('do this, then that'), and a logical connector indicating consequence ('If you study, then you will pass'). It is essential in storytelling, instruction-giving, and logical reasoning.
example_sentence: She finished her homework and then went to the park.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने अपना होमवर्क पूरा किया और फिर पार्क चली गई।

english_meaning: at the present moment; immediately
definition_simple: At the present time or moment; immediately.
definition_detailed: 'Now' refers to the present moment ('do it now'), serves as a discourse marker ('Now, let me explain'), and can indicate an immediate consequence ('Now that you mention it'). It contrasts with 'then' (past or future moment) and 'before' (earlier).
example_sentence: She needs to finish her project right now, urgently.
example_sentence_hindi: उसे अभी तुरंत अपना प्रोजेक्ट खत्म करना है।

english_meaning: to direct eyes toward something; to appear
definition_simple: To direct the eyes at something in order to see it; to seem or appear.
definition_detailed: 'Look' means to direct the eyes ('look at the board'), to appear ('you look tired'), to search ('look for your keys'), and to expect ('I look forward to it'). It forms many phrasal verbs ('look after', 'look up', 'look into'). It differs from 'see' in implying deliberate attention.
example_sentence: Look at the beautiful sunset over the mountains tonight.
example_sentence_hindi: आज रात पहाड़ों के ऊपर सुंदर सूर्यास्त देखो।

english_meaning: and no one or nothing more; solely
definition_simple: No more than; exclusively; just.
definition_detailed: 'Only' is an adverb meaning 'no more than' ('only one'), 'exclusively' ('only you know'), and 'just recently' ('I only just arrived'). As an adjective it means 'single' ('the only one'). Its placement in a sentence can change the meaning significantly: 'I only saw her yesterday' vs. 'I saw only her yesterday'.
example_sentence: She is the only person who truly understands the problem.
example_sentence_hindi: वह एकमात्र व्यक्ति है जो सच में समस्या को समझती है।

english_meaning: to move toward a place or person
definition_simple: To move toward the speaker or to arrive at a place.
definition_detailed: 'Come' indicates movement toward the speaker or the point of reference ('come here'), arrival ('she has come'), and origin ('she comes from India'). It contrasts with 'go' which indicates movement away. It forms many phrasal verbs and idioms ('come up with', 'come across', 'come out').
example_sentence: Please come to my office at ten o'clock tomorrow.
example_sentence_hindi: कृपया कल सुबह दस बजे मेरे कार्यालय में आएं।

english_meaning: belonging to or associated with a thing
definition_simple: Possessive form of it; belonging to a thing or animal.
definition_detailed: 'Its' is the possessive determiner of the neuter pronoun 'it', indicating that something belongs to or is associated with a non-human entity, thing, animal, or idea. Note: it should not be confused with 'it's' (the contraction of 'it is' or 'it has').
example_sentence: The company changed its strategy for the new market.
example_sentence_hindi: कंपनी ने नए बाजार के लिए अपनी रणनीति बदली।

english_meaning: above or across; more than; finished
definition_simple: Above a surface; more than a quantity; completed or finished.
definition_detailed: 'Over' is a preposition and adverb with many meanings: above ('the bridge over the river'), across ('jump over the fence'), more than ('over a hundred'), finished ('the game is over'), and in phrasal verbs ('think it over', 'get over it'). It is one of the most productive words for forming compounds.
example_sentence: The plane flew over the city during the afternoon.
example_sentence_hindi: दोपहर में विमान शहर के ऊपर से उड़ा।

english_meaning: to have thoughts or an opinion about something
definition_simple: To use the mind to consider or form opinions about something.
definition_detailed: 'Think' means to use one's mind to reason ('think about a problem'), to believe or consider ('I think it's right'), to remember ('can't think of her name'), and to expect ('I think it will rain'). It is a stative verb in some uses but can appear in progressive for deliberate thinking.
example_sentence: She needs time to think about the important decision.
example_sentence_hindi: उसे महत्वपूर्ण निर्णय के बारे में सोचने के लिए समय चाहिए।

english_meaning: in addition; besides; too
definition_simple: Used to indicate something additional; as well.
definition_detailed: 'Also' is an adverb meaning 'in addition' or 'too'. It adds information that relates to what was already said ('She is smart; she is also kind'). In more formal writing 'moreover' or 'furthermore' are used, while informally 'too' and 'as well' appear at the end of sentences.
example_sentence: She also wants to learn how to play the piano.
example_sentence_hindi: वह पियानो बजाना भी सीखना चाहती है।

english_meaning: toward the rear; to a former position; the rear part
definition_simple: In or toward the rear; to a former place or state.
definition_detailed: 'Back' functions as an adverb ('go back'), noun ('the back of the chair'), adjective ('back door'), and verb ('to back a car'). It indicates return ('come back'), direction toward the rear ('step back'), past time ('back in 1990'), and appears in many phrasal verbs ('back up', 'look back', 'hold back').
example_sentence: She went back to her hometown after many years abroad.
example_sentence_hindi: वह कई साल विदेश में रहने के बाद अपने गृहनगर वापस गई।

english_meaning: following in time or order
definition_simple: Later in time than something; following in sequence.
definition_detailed: 'After' is a preposition ('after dinner'), conjunction ('after she left'), and adverb ('soon after') indicating time or sequence. It shows that one event follows another. As a prefix it forms words like 'aftermath', 'afternoon', and 'afterthought'.
example_sentence: She always exercises after she finishes her morning coffee.
example_sentence_hindi: वह सुबह की कॉफी खत्म करने के बाद हमेशा व्यायाम करती है।

english_meaning: to employ something for a purpose
definition_simple: To employ or apply something for a purpose or advantage.
definition_detailed: 'Use' as a verb means to employ something ('use a tool'), take advantage of ('use an opportunity'), or consume ('use electricity'). As a noun ('use') it refers to the act of using or its purpose. The phrase 'used to' expresses past habits or states.
example_sentence: She uses modern technology to improve her productivity.
example_sentence_hindi: वह अपनी उत्पादकता बढ़ाने के लिए आधुनिक तकनीक का उपयोग करती है।

english_meaning: the number 2
definition_simple: The number 2; one more than one.
definition_detailed: 'Two' is the cardinal number 2. It counts a pair of items, appears in many expressions ('two-faced', 'in two minds'), and is fundamental to mathematics and language. The adjective form is 'second', and the adverb 'twice' means 'two times'.
example_sentence: She has two younger brothers who live nearby.
example_sentence_hindi: उसके दो छोटे भाई हैं जो पास में रहते हैं।

english_meaning: in what way or manner; to what degree
definition_simple: In what manner, way, or degree; used to ask questions about process.
definition_detailed: 'How' is an interrogative adverb asking about manner ('How did you do it?'), degree ('How tall are you?'), and used in indirect questions ('Tell me how it works'). It is also a relative adverb ('the way how'), an exclamation ('How wonderful!'), and used in greetings ('How are you?').
example_sentence: She explained exactly how the machine works to us.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने हमें बताया कि मशीन ठीक कैसे काम करती है।

english_meaning: belonging to or associated with the speaker and others
definition_simple: Possessive determiner for the speaker and one or more others.
definition_detailed: 'Our' is the first-person plural possessive determiner. It indicates that something belongs to the speaker and at least one other person. The corresponding possessive pronoun is 'ours' and the reflexive is 'ourselves'. It expresses shared ownership or collective identity.
example_sentence: Our team worked hard to finish the project on time.
example_sentence_hindi: हमारी टीम ने समय पर प्रोजेक्ट खत्म करने के लिए कड़ी मेहनत की।

english_meaning: to do a job or task; employment or labor
definition_simple: To do a job, task, or activity; to function properly.
definition_detailed: 'Work' as a verb means to do a job ('work at a hospital'), to function ('the machine works'), or to be effective ('the medicine works'). As a noun it refers to employment ('looking for work'), a task ('the work is done'), and creations ('works of art'). It is central to English vocabulary for labor and effort.
example_sentence: She works very hard to achieve her professional goals.
example_sentence_hindi: वह अपने पेशेवर लक्ष्य हासिल करने के लिए बहुत मेहनत करती है।

english_meaning: coming before all others in time or order
definition_simple: The ordinal number 1; coming before everything else.
definition_detailed: 'First' is the ordinal form of 'one', indicating the position before all others in time, order, or rank. As an adverb it means 'before anything else' ('first, let me explain') or 'for the first time' ('I first met him in Paris'). It is also used in compound expressions like 'first-class' and 'first aid'.
example_sentence: She was the first person to arrive at the meeting.
example_sentence_hindi: वह बैठक में पहुँचने वाली पहली व्यक्ति थी।

english_meaning: in a good or satisfactory manner; also a noun for a water source
definition_simple: In a good or satisfactory way; also used as an interjection.
definition_detailed: 'Well' as an adverb means 'in a good manner' ('she works well'), 'thoroughly' ('mix it well'), or 'considerably' ('well over a hundred'). As an adjective it means 'healthy' ('Are you well?'). As an interjection it introduces or resumes speech ('Well, let me think'). As a noun it is a deep hole for water.
example_sentence: She plays the violin extremely well for a beginner.
example_sentence_hindi: वह एक शुरुआती के लिए बेहद अच्छे से वायलिन बजाती है।

english_meaning: a method, path, or route
definition_simple: A method of doing something; a road or path; a direction.
definition_detailed: 'Way' means a method or manner ('the best way to learn'), a road or path ('way to the station'), a direction ('which way?'), and distance ('a long way off'). It appears in many idiomatic expressions ('by the way', 'no way', 'on the way', 'in the way').
example_sentence: There is always a way to solve every difficult problem.
example_sentence_hindi: हर मुश्किल समस्या को हल करने का कोई न कोई रास्ता होता है।

english_meaning: used for emphasis; also meaning flat or equal
definition_simple: Used to emphasize something surprising; flat, level, or equal.
definition_detailed: 'Even' as an adverb emphasizes something unexpected ('even a child knows this') or adds force ('even better'). As an adjective it means flat ('an even surface'), equal ('an even score'), or calm ('an even temper'). In mathematics it denotes numbers divisible by two.
example_sentence: She didn't give up even when things became very difficult.
example_sentence_hindi: जब हालात बहुत मुश्किल हो गए तब भी उसने हार नहीं मानी।

english_meaning: recently made, discovered, or invented; unfamiliar
definition_simple: Recently made, created, or arrived; not seen before.
definition_detailed: 'New' describes something recently made ('new product'), recently acquired ('new job'), not used before ('new car'), recently learned ('new information'), or currently in a position ('new teacher'). Its antonym is 'old' and comparative is 'newer'. It forms many compound words.
example_sentence: She started a new job at a different company last month.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने पिछले महीने एक अलग कंपनी में नई नौकरी शुरू की।

english_meaning: to have a desire for something
definition_simple: To feel a desire or wish for something.
definition_detailed: 'Want' expresses desire ('I want coffee'), need ('the job wants skill'), and in formal English means to lack ('in want of guidance'). It is commonly followed by a noun or an infinitive ('want to go'). It is a stative verb and not typically used in progressive form, though informally 'wanting' does occur.
example_sentence: She wants to become a successful doctor in the future.
example_sentence_hindi: वह भविष्य में एक सफल डॉक्टर बनना चाहती है।

english_meaning: for the reason that; since
definition_simple: Used to give the reason for something.
definition_detailed: 'Because' is a subordinating conjunction that introduces a clause explaining the reason for the main clause ('She left because she was tired'). It answers the question 'why?'. It should not begin a sentence in formal writing according to traditional style guides, though this rule is widely relaxed in modern English.
example_sentence: She stayed home because she was feeling very unwell.
example_sentence_hindi: वह घर पर रही क्योंकि वह बहुत अस्वस्थ महसूस कर रही थी।

english_meaning: used in questions and negatives; one or some of whatever kind
definition_simple: Used to refer to one or some of whatever type, often in questions or negatives.
definition_detailed: 'Any' is used in questions ('Do you have any questions?'), negatives ('I don't have any'), and affirmative sentences with special meaning ('Take any seat you like'). It expresses indifference to which specific item or person. As an adverb it emphasizes comparison ('not any better').
example_sentence: She couldn't find any good restaurants near the hotel.
example_sentence_hindi: उसे होटल के पास कोई भी अच्छा रेस्टोरेंट नहीं मिला।

english_meaning: plural form of this; referring to nearby things
definition_simple: Refers to multiple specific things or people nearby or just mentioned.
definition_detailed: 'These' is the plural of 'this', used as a demonstrative determiner ('these books') and pronoun ('I'll take these'). It indicates items that are physically close to the speaker or have just been mentioned. It contrasts with 'those', which refers to things farther away.
example_sentence: These new ideas could help improve our current system.
example_sentence_hindi: ये नए विचार हमारी मौजूदा प्रणाली को बेहतर बनाने में मदद कर सकते हैं।

english_meaning: to transfer something to someone
definition_simple: To transfer possession of something to another person.
definition_detailed: 'Give' means to transfer something to someone ('give a gift'), to produce or provide ('give a speech', 'give light'), and to yield or concede ('give way'). It is one of the most common ditransitive verbs in English (taking both direct and indirect objects). Its irregular past tense is 'gave'.
example_sentence: She gave her younger sister a beautiful birthday gift.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने अपनी छोटी बहन को एक सुंदर जन्मदिन का उपहार दिया।

english_meaning: a period of 24 hours; the time of light
definition_simple: A period of 24 hours; the time between sunrise and sunset.
definition_detailed: 'Day' refers to the 24-hour period ('three days'), the period of daylight (contrasted with night), a specific date ('on that day'), and an era or period ('in my day'). It is fundamental to measuring time and appears in countless compound words and expressions.
example_sentence: She spends every day trying to learn something new.
example_sentence_hindi: वह हर दिन कुछ नया सीखने की कोशिश करती है।

english_meaning: the greatest in amount or degree; the majority
definition_simple: The greatest quantity, extent, or degree; the majority.
definition_detailed: 'Most' is the superlative of 'many' and 'much'. As a determiner it indicates the greatest quantity ('most people'). As an adverb it forms superlatives ('most beautiful'). As a noun it means the greatest part ('most of the work'). The phrase 'at most' means not more than; 'for the most part' means mainly.
example_sentence: Most students passed the difficult exam with good grades.
example_sentence_hindi: अधिकांश छात्रों ने अच्छे अंकों से कठिन परीक्षा पास की।

english_meaning: object form of we
definition_simple: Used as the object of a verb or preposition to refer to the speaker and others.
definition_detailed: 'Us' is the first-person plural object pronoun. It is used as the direct object ('She helped us'), indirect object ('She gave us the news'), and object of a preposition ('for us'). In informal speech it sometimes replaces 'we' as subject, though this is non-standard.
example_sentence: She invited all of us to her graduation celebration.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने हम सभी को अपने स्नातक समारोह में आमंत्रित किया।

english_meaning: of an exceptionally high quality; large in size or extent
definition_simple: Of a very high quality or importance; large in scale or significance.
definition_detailed: 'Great' means very good ('a great idea'), large ('a great distance'), important ('a great moment'), and skillful ('a great artist'). It is also used in family relationships ('great-grandfather') and as an enthusiastic affirmation. Historically it also meant 'physically large'.
example_sentence: She has a great passion for music and creative arts.
example_sentence_hindi: उसे संगीत और रचनात्मक कलाओं के प्रति बहुत जुनून है।

english_meaning: in the space separating two things
definition_simple: In the space, time, or gap that separates two things or people.
definition_detailed: 'Between' is a preposition indicating position or time separating two distinct entities ('between the chairs', 'between Monday and Friday'). It can also show connection or relationship ('an agreement between them'). For more than two items 'among' is traditionally preferred, though this distinction is relaxing.
example_sentence: She sat between her two best friends during the lunch break.
example_sentence_hindi: लंच ब्रेक में वह अपने दो सबसे अच्छे दोस्तों के बीच बैठी।

english_meaning: to require something as necessary
definition_simple: To require something as essential or necessary.
definition_detailed: 'Need' as a main verb means to require ('I need water'). As a modal it negates obligation ('you needn't go'). As a noun it refers to necessity ('a basic need'). Unlike 'want', 'need' implies necessity rather than desire. It can be used with or without 'to' in negative and question contexts.
example_sentence: She needs to practice every day to improve her skills.
example_sentence_hindi: अपने कौशल को सुधारने के लिए उसे हर रोज अभ्यास करना चाहिए।

english_meaning: of more than average size, amount, or extent
definition_simple: Greater than normal in size, extent, or amount.
definition_detailed: 'Large' describes size ('a large room'), amount ('a large number'), or scale ('a large organization'). It is generally synonymous with 'big' but 'large' is preferred in formal contexts and 'big' in informal ones. 'At large' means free or unconstrained; 'by and large' means generally.
example_sentence: She lives in a large house with a beautiful garden.
example_sentence_hindi: वह एक बड़े घर में रहती है जिसमें एक सुंदर बगीचा है।

english_meaning: many times; frequently
definition_simple: Happening many times; with regularity.
definition_detailed: 'Often' means 'frequently' or 'many times'. It indicates regularity in occurrence and typically appears before the main verb or after auxiliaries. It contrasts with 'sometimes' (occasionally), 'rarely' (seldom), and 'never'. The comparative form is 'more often' and superlative 'most often'.
example_sentence: She often visits the local library to read new books.
example_sentence_hindi: वह नई किताबें पढ़ने के लिए अक्सर स्थानीय पुस्तकालय जाती है।

english_meaning: the end part of the arm with fingers
definition_simple: The body part at the end of the arm used for grasping and touching.
definition_detailed: 'Hand' is primarily the body part at the end of the human arm used for grasping and manipulation. As a verb it means to give ('hand me the book'). It appears in dozens of idioms ('hand in hand', 'out of hand', 'first-hand', 'give a hand'). The plural 'hands' often means workers or crew members.
example_sentence: She raised her hand to ask the teacher a question.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने शिक्षक से एक प्रश्न पूछने के लिए हाथ उठाया।

english_meaning: of great vertical extent; elevated in position or level
definition_simple: At or to a great height; of a level greater than normal.
definition_detailed: 'High' describes vertical extent ('a high mountain'), level ('high temperature'), rank ('high office'), quality ('high standard'), or intensity ('high hopes'). As an adverb it means 'to a great height'. Its comparative is 'higher' and superlative 'highest'. The noun form is 'height'.
example_sentence: She climbed to a very high point on the mountain trail.
example_sentence_hindi: वह पहाड़ी रास्ते पर बहुत ऊँचे बिंदु तक चढ़ी।

english_meaning: a particular position or area; to put something somewhere
definition_simple: A specific location or area; to put something in a position.
definition_detailed: 'Place' as a noun means a specific location ('a quiet place'), a position in a sequence ('second place'), or a role ('in place of'). As a verb it means to put something in position ('place the book here'). It appears in many expressions ('take place', 'in the first place', 'out of place').
example_sentence: This is a beautiful place to relax on a quiet afternoon.
example_sentence_hindi: यह शांत दोपहर में आराम करने के लिए एक सुंदर जगह है।

english_meaning: to keep in one's grasp; to contain
definition_simple: To keep or carry in one's hand or arms; to keep something in a position.
definition_detailed: 'Hold' means to grasp ('hold a cup'), contain ('the bag holds ten items'), organize ('hold a meeting'), remain valid ('the rule holds'), and wait ('hold on'). Its irregular past tense is 'held'. It forms many phrasal verbs ('hold back', 'hold on', 'hold up') and appears in many idioms.
example_sentence: She held her little daughter's hand as they crossed the road.
example_sentence_hindi: सड़क पार करते समय उसने अपनी छोटी बेटी का हाथ थामे रखा।

english_meaning: to rotate or change direction; a rotation or opportunity
definition_simple: To move in a circle or change direction; an opportunity or chance.
definition_detailed: 'Turn' as a verb means to rotate ('turn the wheel'), change direction ('turn left'), change state ('turn cold'), or transform ('turn into'). As a noun it means a rotation, a person's opportunity ('your turn'), and a bend in a road. It forms many phrasal verbs ('turn up', 'turn down', 'turn into').
example_sentence: Please turn left at the traffic light on the main road.
example_sentence_hindi: मुख्य सड़क पर ट्रैफिक लाइट पर बाईं ओर मुड़ें।

english_meaning: a small number of; not many
definition_simple: A small number of things; not many but more than one or two.
definition_detailed: 'Few' as a determiner means 'a small number' ('few people came'). 'A few' means 'some but not many' and is positive, while 'few' without the article implies less than expected. In formal contexts 'fewer' (countable) contrasts with 'less' (uncountable). 'Quite a few' means 'a fairly large number'.
example_sentence: Only a few students managed to complete the project early.
example_sentence_hindi: केवल कुछ छात्र ही परियोजना को जल्दी पूरा कर पाए।

english_meaning: at no time; not ever
definition_simple: At no time in the past, present, or future; not ever.
definition_detailed: 'Never' is a strong negation adverb meaning 'at no time'. It is stronger than 'not' and 'hardly ever'. 'Never' is placed before the main verb but after auxiliaries. It is used for absolute denial ('I never lie'), strong advice ('never give up'), and emphasis ('never in my life').
example_sentence: She never gives up on her goals, no matter how hard they seem.
example_sentence_hindi: वह कभी हार नहीं मानती, चाहे उसके लक्ष्य कितने भी कठिन क्यों न लगें।

english_meaning: having lived or existed for a long time; not new
definition_simple: Having existed or been in use for a long time; advanced in age.
definition_detailed: 'Old' describes age ('an old man'), duration ('an old tradition'), and familiar connection ('an old friend'). Its comparative is 'older' (or formally 'elder' for family relations) and superlative is 'oldest/eldest'. It contrasts with 'new' (things) and 'young' (people).
example_sentence: She learned a lot from talking to the old villagers.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने पुराने गाँव वालों से बात करके बहुत कुछ सीखा।

english_meaning: past tense of find; or to establish an institution
definition_simple: Past tense of 'find'; also means to establish or start an institution.
definition_detailed: 'Found' is the past tense and past participle of 'find' ('I found my keys'). It is also a separate verb meaning to establish or create an institution ('found a company'). These two verbs have different pronunciations in some dialects but are spelled the same in the past tense.
example_sentence: She finally found the answer she had been looking for so long.
example_sentence_hindi: आखिरकार उसे वह जवाब मिल गया जिसे वह इतने समय से ढूंढ रही थी।

english_meaning: of great length or duration
definition_simple: Extending for a great distance or a great amount of time.
definition_detailed: 'Long' as an adjective describes physical length ('a long road') or duration ('a long time'). As an adverb it means 'for a long time' ('She waited long'). As a verb it means 'to yearn' ('I long to travel'). The noun form is 'length'. Many compounds exist: 'long-term', 'long-distance', 'lifelong'.
example_sentence: She has been waiting for this long-awaited opportunity for years.
example_sentence_hindi: वह इस बहुप्रतीक्षित अवसर का वर्षों से इंतजार कर रही थी।

english_meaning: of great size; important or significant
definition_simple: Of a large size; important or having a great impact.
definition_detailed: 'Big' is the informal counterpart of 'large' or 'great'. It describes size ('a big house'), importance ('a big decision'), scale ('a big company'), and emotional intensity ('a big hug'). It is more common in informal speech, while 'large' is preferred in formal writing.
example_sentence: She has big dreams and works hard every single day.
example_sentence_hindi: उसके बड़े सपने हैं और वह हर एक दिन मेहनत करती है।

english_meaning: toward or in a lower position
definition_simple: Moving or directed toward a lower position or level.
definition_detailed: 'Down' indicates movement to a lower position, reduction in level, or failure. It forms many phrasal verbs ('sit down', 'write down', 'break down', 'come down'). As a preposition it means 'along' ('down the street'). As an adjective it means 'sad' or 'not working'. As a noun it refers to soft feathers.
example_sentence: She walked slowly down the stairs to the kitchen below.
example_sentence_hindi: वह धीरे-धीरे नीचे रसोई तक सीढ़ियाँ उतरी।

english_meaning: expressing possibility or permission
definition_simple: Used to express possibility, permission, or wishes.
definition_detailed: 'May' is a modal auxiliary expressing possibility ('it may rain'), permission in formal contexts ('may I come in?'), and wishes ('may you be well'). It is more formal than 'can' for permission. Its past tense is 'might'. In formal writing 'may' is preferred over 'can' for permission.
example_sentence: You may leave the room when you have finished your work.
example_sentence_hindi: जब आप अपना काम पूरा कर लें तो आप कमरे से जा सकते हैं।

english_meaning: continuing to be; up to this time; motionless
definition_simple: Up to and including this moment; without movement; even so.
definition_detailed: 'Still' as an adverb means 'continuing up to the present' ('she is still here'), 'even so' ('still, I believe it'), and 'even more' ('still better'). As an adjective it means motionless or calm. As a noun it refers to a device for distilling liquids. In comparisons 'still' emphasizes the comparative.
example_sentence: She is still working at the same company after ten years.
example_sentence_hindi: दस साल बाद भी वह उसी कंपनी में काम कर रही है।

english_meaning: belonging to a specified person; to possess
definition_simple: Belonging to oneself; to have as property.
definition_detailed: 'Own' as an adjective emphasizes that something belongs to the person mentioned ('my own idea'). As a verb it means to possess ('I own a car'). 'On your own' means alone. 'Come into one's own' means to demonstrate one's abilities. 'Hold your own' means to maintain your position.
example_sentence: She started her own business after years of experience.
example_sentence_hindi: वह वर्षों के अनुभव के बाद अपना खुद का व्यवसाय शुरू किया।

english_meaning: morally good; correct; the opposite of left
definition_simple: Correct or true; the opposite of left; morally acceptable.
definition_detailed: 'Right' serves as an adjective ('the right answer'), noun ('human rights', 'the right side'), adverb ('turn right', 'right away'), and interjection ('Right!', agreeing). It has two main meanings: correctness/morality and the direction opposite to left. These meanings are etymologically distinct.
example_sentence: She made the right decision by choosing to study abroad.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने विदेश में पढ़ाई करने का सही निर्णय लिया।

english_meaning: used to express obligation, expectation, or advice
definition_simple: Used to express duty, expectation, or advice about what is right.
definition_detailed: 'Should' is the past tense of 'shall' but functions mainly as a modal auxiliary expressing obligation or duty ('you should work hard'), expectation ('it should be ready by now'), advice ('you should see a doctor'), and conditional ('if you should see her'). It is less strong than 'must'.
example_sentence: You should always be honest with people around you.
example_sentence_hindi: आपको हमेशा अपने आस-पास के लोगों के साथ ईमानदार रहना चाहिए।

english_meaning: during the time that; at the same time as; although
definition_simple: During a period of time; at the same time as something else.
definition_detailed: 'While' as a conjunction indicates simultaneous events ('she sang while she cooked'), contrast ('while I understand, I disagree'), and duration ('wait a while'). As a noun it refers to a period of time. As a verb it means to pass time idly ('while away the hours').
example_sentence: She listened to music while completing her morning exercise routine.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने अपनी सुबह की व्यायाम दिनचर्या पूरी करते हुए संगीत सुना।

english_meaning: plural form of that; referring to distant things
definition_simple: Refers to multiple specific people or things farther away or previously mentioned.
definition_detailed: 'Those' is the plural of 'that', used as a demonstrative determiner ('those books') and pronoun ('I prefer those'). It indicates items that are farther from the speaker or have been previously discussed, contrasting with 'these' which indicates items nearby.
example_sentence: Those were the best years of her entire professional life.
example_sentence_hindi: वे उसके पूरे पेशेवर जीवन के सबसे अच्छे साल थे।

english_meaning: the one and the other; the two
definition_simple: Used to refer to two people or things, considered together.
definition_detailed: 'Both' indicates two people or things together ('both students passed'). As a conjunction in 'both...and', it emphasizes that each of two things is true ('both clever and kind'). It is used with plural nouns and verbs. It contrasts with 'either' (one or the other) and 'neither' (not one or the other).
example_sentence: Both candidates presented very strong arguments in the debate.
example_sentence_hindi: दोनों उम्मीदवारों ने बहस में बहुत मजबूत तर्क प्रस्तुत किए।

english_meaning: despite that; in whatever way
definition_simple: Used to introduce a contrast or exception to what has just been said.
definition_detailed: 'However' is a conjunctive adverb introducing contrast ('She was tired; however, she kept working') and a relative adverb meaning 'in whatever way' ('Do it however you like'). As a sentence connector it is separated by semicolons or periods, unlike 'but' which connects within a sentence.
example_sentence: The task was difficult; however, she completed it on time.
example_sentence_hindi: काम मुश्किल था; हालाँकि, उसने इसे समय पर पूरा किया।

english_meaning: the number 3
definition_simple: The number 3; one more than two.
definition_detailed: 'Three' is the cardinal number 3. It counts three items and appears in many cultural and symbolic uses: 'three-dimensional', 'the rule of three' in writing, 'three strikes'. The ordinal form is 'third' and the adverb form is 'thrice' (three times).
example_sentence: She has three older siblings who all live in different cities.
example_sentence_hindi: उसके तीन बड़े भाई-बहन हैं जो सभी अलग-अलग शहरों में रहते हैं।

english_meaning: away from a place or position; not functioning
definition_simple: Away from a surface or position; not operating or functioning.
definition_detailed: 'Off' indicates separation ('take off your shoes'), removal ('off the hook'), distance ('far off'), inactivity ('switch off'), cancellation ('the event is off'), and is extremely productive in phrasal verbs ('put off', 'take off', 'turn off', 'set off', 'go off'). Its antonym is 'on'.
example_sentence: She turned off the lights before leaving the room tonight.
example_sentence_hindi: आज रात कमरा छोड़ने से पहले उसने लाइटें बंद कर दीं।

english_meaning: at all times; on every occasion; repeatedly
definition_simple: On every occasion; all the time; constantly.
definition_detailed: 'Always' is the strongest frequency adverb, meaning 'on all occasions' or 'at all times'. It appears before the main verb ('I always eat breakfast') but after auxiliaries ('She has always worked hard'). It expresses habit, certainty, and permanence. Its antonym is 'never' and it contrasts with 'usually', 'sometimes'.
example_sentence: She always arrives at work before anyone else in the office.
example_sentence_hindi: वह हमेशा दफ्तर में किसी भी और से पहले काम पर पहुँचती है।

english_meaning: to give the impression of being something
definition_simple: To give the impression or appearance of having a certain quality.
definition_detailed: 'Seem' is a linking verb expressing appearance or impression ('she seems happy', 'it seems like rain'). Unlike 'be', 'seem' implies the speaker is not certain of the true state. It is followed by an adjective ('seems tired'), a noun phrase ('seems a good idea'), or an infinitive ('seems to be working').
example_sentence: Everything seems to be working perfectly in the new system.
example_sentence_hindi: नई प्रणाली में सब कुछ बिल्कुल ठीक काम करता लगता है।

english_meaning: from a past time until now; because
definition_simple: From a point in the past until now; as a result of the fact that.
definition_detailed: 'Since' as a preposition marks a starting point continuing to now ('since 2010'). As a conjunction it introduces a time clause ('since she left') or a causal clause ('since you insist'). It is used with perfect tenses when marking duration from the past. It contrasts with 'for' which marks duration only.
example_sentence: She has been learning Spanish since she was a young child.
example_sentence_hindi: वह बचपन से स्पैनिश सीख रही है।

english_meaning: to make visible; to demonstrate or prove
definition_simple: To make something visible or known; to demonstrate or prove.
definition_detailed: 'Show' as a verb means to make visible ('show your work'), demonstrate ('show how it works'), prove ('evidence shows'), and lead ('show someone around'). As a noun it refers to a performance or presentation. Its irregular past participle is 'shown' (also 'showed' as past participle).
example_sentence: She will show the new students around the campus tomorrow.
example_sentence_hindi: वह कल नए छात्रों को परिसर में घुमाएगी।

english_meaning: a surface of an object; a position or perspective
definition_simple: One of the surfaces, edges, or aspects of something; a perspective.
definition_detailed: 'Side' means a surface ('the side of the box'), position ('the left side'), team or party ('whose side are you on?'), an aspect ('the funny side'), and in math/geometry a line of a shape. In idioms: 'on the safe side', 'take sides', 'side by side'.
example_sentence: She always looks at both sides of every argument carefully.
example_sentence_hindi: वह हमेशा हर तर्क के दोनों पक्षों को ध्यान से देखती है।

english_meaning: present participle of be; existence; a living thing
definition_simple: The present participle of 'be'; existence; a living creature.
definition_detailed: 'Being' is the present participle of 'be', used in continuous tenses ('she is being careful') and passive constructions ('being chosen'). As a noun it refers to existence ('the nature of being') or a living creature ('a human being'). In gerund use: 'being late is rude'.
example_sentence: Being kind to others is one of the most important values.
example_sentence_hindi: दूसरों के प्रति दयालु होना सबसे महत्वपूर्ण मूल्यों में से एक है।

english_meaning: after the expected or proper time; toward the end of a period
definition_simple: After the expected time; happening or arriving after the usual time.
definition_detailed: 'Late' as an adjective means after the expected time ('late arrival'), toward the end of a period ('late afternoon', 'late 19th century'), or recently deceased ('the late president'). As an adverb it means after the scheduled time ('she arrived late'). The comparative is 'later' and superlative 'latest'.
example_sentence: She arrived late to the meeting because of heavy traffic.
example_sentence_hindi: भारी यातायात के कारण वह बैठक में देर से पहुँची।

english_meaning: immediately following in time, order, or space
definition_simple: Coming immediately after in sequence, time, or space.
definition_detailed: 'Next' indicates the item immediately following in a sequence ('the next page'), in time ('next week'), or in space ('the next house'). It can also mean 'nearest' ('the next town'). The expression 'next to' means 'beside' or 'almost' ('next to nothing'). As an adverb it means 'after this'.
example_sentence: She will start her new course next Monday morning.
example_sentence_hindi: वह अगले सोमवार सुबह अपना नया पाठ्यक्रम शुरू करेगी।

english_meaning: not closed; accessible; to make accessible
definition_simple: Not closed or sealed; accessible; to make something accessible.
definition_detailed: 'Open' as an adjective means not closed ('open door'), accessible ('open to ideas'), frank ('open discussion'), and unsettled ('open question'). As a verb it means to make accessible ('open a window'). Many expressions: 'open-minded', 'in the open', 'open to interpretation', 'an open secret'.
example_sentence: She opened the window to let in some fresh morning air.
example_sentence_hindi: उसने सुबह की ताजी हवा लेने के लिए खिड़की खोली।

english_meaning: to be in an upright position; to tolerate
definition_simple: To be on one's feet in an upright position; to tolerate or endure.
definition_detailed: 'Stand' means to be upright on one's feet ('stand up'), to remain stationary ('stand aside'), to tolerate ('I can't stand the noise'), to be valid ('the rule still stands'), and to represent ('the letters stand for'). Its irregular past tense is 'stood'. Many phrasal verbs: 'stand up', 'stand for', 'stand out'.
example_sentence: She stood at the podium and delivered a powerful speech.
example_sentence_hindi: वह मंच पर खड़ी हुई और एक शक्तिशाली भाषण दिया।

english_meaning: to look at and comprehend written text
definition_simple: To look at and understand written or printed words.
definition_detailed: 'Read' means to comprehend written text ('read a book'), interpret ('read a situation'), recite aloud ('read to children'), and register on a device ('the meter reads 100'). Its past tense and past participle 'read' is pronounced /rɛd/ — the same spelling but different pronunciation. 'Well-read' means widely educated through books.
example_sentence: She reads at least one new book every week without fail.
example_sentence_hindi: वह बिना नागा किए हर हफ्ते कम से कम एक नई किताब पढ़ती है।

english_meaning: to perceive sound with the ears
definition_simple: To perceive sounds with the ears; to receive information.
definition_detailed: 'Hear' means to perceive sound ('I can hear music'), to receive information ('I hear she's leaving'), to listen to and consider ('the judge heard the case'), and to understand ('I hear you'). Unlike 'listen', 'hear' is involuntary. Its irregular past tense is 'heard'.
